commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for after-fix real behavior proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to perform while the patch itself has no blocking source finding.; Cleared: The patch only changes TypeScript runtime logic and tests around abort classification; I found no dependency, workflow, secret, package, or supply-chain surface change. (sha=f7d1aa864bd3b6ef49b78054a174d0d53fcce732)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model fallback and external-run failure handling so provider-side AbortErrors can fall through to fallback/error replies while real reply-operation cancellations remain silent.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +91. Total +118 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source reproduction: current main rethrows non-timeout AbortErrors before fallback and later silences generic group/channel failures. I did not execute a live provider/channel repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for after-fix real behavior proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to perform while the patch itself has no blocking source finding.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`externalAbort: false` in trajectory). The existing code treated *any* AbortError as a user/gateway cancellation, causing a silent failure chain in group/channel sessions.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `externalAbort: false`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = true (name === "AbortError"), `normalizedFailover` = null → **throws**, skipping all fallback candidates
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: not a user/restart/draining abort → `buildExternalRunFailureReply` → `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → **`SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`**
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Real behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation (private details redacted):

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{
  "type": "trace.artifacts",
  "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": {
    "finalStatus": "error",
    "aborted": true,
    "externalAbort": false,
    "promptError": "This operation was aborted",
    "promptErrorSource": "prompt"
  }
}
```

**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session, subsequent message — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{
  "type": "trace.artifacts",
  "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": {
    "finalStatus": "error",
    "aborted": true,
    "externalAbort": false,
    "promptError": "This operation was aborted",
    "promptErrorSource": "prompt"
  }
}
```

Both events: `externalAbort: false` confirms no external signal fired. Both resulted in `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs (feishu channel, group conversation).

After the fix, subsequent messages in the same topic replied normally — confirming the session was not permanently broken, only every individual turn that hit the provider-side abort was silenced.

## Fix (addresses ClawSweeper P2 — no-fallback path also fixed)

**Two-part fix covering both the fallback and no-fallback cases:**

### Part 1 — `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`
Gate `shouldRethrowAbort` rethrow on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`:
```diff
-    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
+    // Only rethrow when external signal has actually fired (user/gateway cancel).
+    // Provider-side AbortErrors (no signal) fall through to fallback/error reply.
+    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
       throw err;
     }
```
→ With fallback configured: error enters fallback loop, tries next model.

### Part 2 — `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
Clear `isGenericRunnerFailure` flag for provider-side AbortErrors before `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`:
```diff
+    // Provider-side AbortError must not be silenced in group/channel sessions.
+    isGenericRunnerFailure:
+      (externalRunFailureReply?.isGenericRunnerFailure ?? false) &&
+      !(isAbortError(err) && !(params.replyOperation?.result?.kind === "aborted")),
-    isGenericRunnerFailure: externalRunFailureReply?.isGenericRunnerFailure ?? false,
```
→ Without fallback (or all candidates exhausted): user sees ⚠️ error message instead of silence.

## Tests

### `model-fallback.test.ts` (78 tests, all pass)
- Updated `"does not fall back on user aborts"` → passes a pre-aborted signal (correct precondition for genuine user cancel)
- Added `"falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external abort signal)"` — proves fallback path taken

### `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` (189 tests, all pass — +6 new)
- Added `it.each(NON_DIRECT_FAILURE_SURFACE_CASES)("surfaces visible error (not SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN) for provider-side AbortError in $label chats")` — 6 cases covering Discord group/channel, Slack channel, Telegram group, WhatsApp group, Teams channel

Both test files pass clean with `vitest run`.
target.author: shengting

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
